questions.question: Design a 2:1 multiplexer using Verilog. A 2:1 multiplexer selects one of its two input lines (A or B) to pass on to its output. The select line (S) determines which input line to select. For example, if S = 1, then A will be passed on to the output, and if S = 0, then B will be passed on to the output. Write the Verilog code for the above 2:1 multiplexer.

Design a 3:2 multiplexer using Verilog. A 3:2 multiplexer selects one of its two input lines (A or B) to pass on to its output, along with information from a third input line (C) to select which of the two input lines to pass on. The select lines (S and T) determine which input line to select. For example, if S = 1 and T = 1, then A will be passed on to the output, and if S = 0 and T = 1, then C will be used to select between A and B. Write the Verilog code for the above 3:2 multiplexer.

Design a 4:1 multiplexer using Verilog. A 4:1 multiplexer selects one of its four input lines (A, B, C, or D) to pass on to its output. The select lines (S1 and S0) determine which input line to select. For example, if S1 = 1 and S0 = 1, then A will be passed on to the output, and if S1 = 0 and S0 = 1, then B will be passed on to the output. Write the Verilog code for the above 4:1 multiplexer.
